file_title: Vehicle Security Assessment: Diagnostic Port Vulnerabilities
introduction: Given the evolving landscape of vehicle security, it's vital we understand the risks posed to our electric vehicles. Today, we'll delve into the physical access attacks via diagnostic ports (OBD-II) experienced in 2025.

subsections.heading: Vehicle Cybersecurity Landscape in 2025
subsections.content:
• An estimated 90% of new vehicles in 2025 are connected, increasing the overall cybersecurity risk
• Major automakers, including Elexion, have established dedicated cybersecurity divisions
• Cybersecurity investments in the automotive sector have reached an all-time high of $1.5 billion in 2025
• The most common attacks include ransomware, data theft, and manipulation of vehicle control systems
• Regulatory bodies are pressing for stricter cybersecurity measures in connected vehicles
• Partnership between automakers and cybersecurity firms have increased by 30% since 2020
• The average cost of a car cybersecurity breach is estimated to be $3.6 million
subsections.justification: Provides general industry trends and statistics, but does not address OBD-II physical access attacks on Elexion vehicles.

subsections.heading: Incidents of OBD-II Physical Access Attacks
subsections.content:
• OBD-II physical access attacks have been on the rise globally, with a 15% increase in reported incidents since 2020
• These types of attacks allow for unauthorized control of vehicle systems
• Auto manufacturers are exploring hardware and software solutions to mitigate these attacks
• A significant number of these attacks are targeted at commercial fleets
• The automotive industry is lobbying for stricter penalties for OBD-II port attacks
• The average financial loss due to an OBD-II physical access attack is estimated to be $2.2 million
• In 2024, a high-profile OBD-II attack led to a 10% drop in the affected automaker's share price
subsections.justification: Focuses on general industry statistics and incidents, but does not mention specific OBD-II attacks on Elexion vehicles in 2025.

subsections.heading: Impact and Mitigation Strategies for OBD-II Vulnerabilities
subsections.content:
• OBD-II vulnerabilities can lead to theft of sensitive data and unauthorized control of vehicle systems
• Manufacturers are employing encryption and secure coding practices to protect OBD-II ports
• Industry-wide adoption of a standardized security framework for OBD-II ports is being discussed
• Mitigation strategies include periodic security audits, vulnerability assessments, and penetration testing
• Industry collaboration and information sharing are key to addressing OBD-II vulnerabilities
• Automotive cybersecurity insurance is becoming increasingly important as a risk management tool
• Third-party cybersecurity firms are providing specialized services to secure OBD-II ports
subsections.justification: Discusses mitigation strategies and impacts of OBD-II vulnerabilities, but does not provide the number of Elexion vehicles affected in 2025.

conclusion: It is clear that vehicle security, particularly concerning diagnostic port vulnerabilities, is a key area for our attention. Moving forward, we need to continue investing in robust security measures to ensure the safety and trust of our customers.
